Who We Are

Our History & Mission

AL BAHAJA STUDENTS ASSOCIATION was founded with a singular vision — to empower students through unity, leadership, and academic excellence. "Al Bahaja" (الـبهجة) meaning joy and radiance in Arabic, reflects our commitment to bringing brightness and purpose to student life.

Since our inception, we have been a cornerstone of student development — organizing events, welfare programs, and community initiatives that uplift every member. Our executive committees across consecutive years have upheld the highest standards of governance and service.

We believe in the transformative power of organized student leadership. Every program we run, every initiative we lead, and every gathering we host is designed to foster bonds that last a lifetime and skills that shape future leaders.

AL BAHAJA

STUDENTS ASSOCIATION

© AL BAHAJA STUDENTS ASSOCIATION. All rights reserved.

Terms Privacy Contact
Terms & Conditions

Last updated: January 2025

1. Acceptance of Terms

By accessing the AL BAHAJA STUDENTS ASSOCIATION website and participating in its activities, you agree to abide by these terms and conditions in full.

2. Membership

Membership is open to all eligible students as defined by the association's constitution. Members are expected to uphold the values of respect, integrity, and academic excellence.

3. Use of Content

All content on this website including logos, images, text, and media belongs to AL BAHAJA STUDENTS ASSOCIATION. Reproduction without prior written consent is prohibited.

4. Code of Conduct

Members must conduct themselves respectfully in all association activities, both online and offline. Harassment, discrimination, or misconduct of any form will result in immediate suspension.

5. Events & Activities

Participation in events is subject to availability and the association reserves the right to modify, postpone, or cancel events at its discretion.

6. Liability

The association shall not be held liable for any personal injury, loss, or damage arising from participation in events unless caused by the association's direct negligence.

7. Amendments

These terms may be updated periodically. Continued use of association resources implies acceptance of the updated terms.

Privacy Policy

Last updated: January 2025

1. Information We Collect

We collect information that you voluntarily provide when contacting us, registering for events, or joining the association — including your name, email address, and student ID.

2. How We Use Information

Your information is used solely to communicate association updates, manage membership records, and coordinate events. We do not sell or share your data with third parties.

3. Data Storage

All personal data is stored securely and accessible only to authorized committee members. Data is retained only for as long as necessary for its purpose.

4. Photographs & Media

Photos and videos taken at association events may be published on our platforms. If you wish to have your image removed, please contact us immediately.

5. Your Rights

You have the right to access, correct, or request deletion of your personal data held by the association at any time.

6. Contact

For any privacy concerns, please contact us at albahajastudents@gmail.com.

Cookie Policy
What Are Cookies?

Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help improve user experience and functionality.

Cookies We Use

This website uses minimal essential cookies for navigation and session management only. We do not use tracking or advertising cookies.

Managing Cookies

You can control or delete cookies through your browser settings at any time without affecting your ability to use the site.

Code of Conduct
Our Values

AL BAHAJA STUDENTS ASSOCIATION is committed to fostering an inclusive, respectful, and empowering environment for all members.

Expected Behavior

All members are expected to treat each other with dignity and respect, contribute positively to association activities, and uphold the reputation of the association at all times.

Prohibited Behavior

Harassment, bullying, discrimination on any basis, use of inappropriate language, and conduct that brings the association into disrepute are strictly prohibited.

Reporting

Any violations should be reported to the General Secretary or President in writing. All reports will be treated confidentially and investigated fairly.

Consequences

Violations may result in warnings, suspension, or permanent removal from the association depending on severity, as determined by the executive committee.
